summary: IDF reported killing a terrorist planning attacks in Gaza and eliminated a Hamas security official. Hezbollah launched rockets into the Galilee, with one intercepted and another striking near troops, while Israel struck Hezbollah infrastructure after an anti‑tank missile hit an IDF tank. Multiple airstrikes targeted militant leaders and sites across Gaza and southern Lebanon, prompting civilian evacuations.
key_points: IDF killed a terrorist planning imminent attacks in Gaza and targeting Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24155).
Hezbollah launched two rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; one was intercepted, the other landed near forces with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58408).
Israel air‑struck Hezbollah engineering unit commander and a rocket launcher in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Sirens warned of rocket fire from Lebanon in several Galilee Panhandle communities (Source: @manniefabian/58406).
An anti‑tank missile hit an IDF Golani tank north of the Litani River, killing Capt. Itan Samual Lemberg; Israel responded with airstrikes on Hezbollah infrastructure (Source: @inon_yttach/8592).
Israel and Shin Bet eliminated senior Hamas security officials in Gaza, including Hassan Rabaḥ (Source: @almogboker78/17317).
Hezbollah denied accusations of targeting UN peacekeeping headquarters, calling the claim a lie (Source: @presstv/192727).

sections.executive_summary: IDF killed a terrorist planning attacks in Gaza and eliminated a senior Hamas security official. Hezbollah launched rockets into the Galilee, with one intercepted and another striking near troops. Israel conducted airstrikes on Hezbollah infrastructure after an anti‑tank missile hit an IDF tank.

points: No direct operational reports involving Iran were present in the sourced messages.
IDF announced the elimination of a terrorist who was planning immediate attacks in Gaza and had also attempted to direct attacks inside Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24155).

The Israeli Air Force struck Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon following an anti‑tank missile attack that killed Capt. Itan Samual Lemberg near the Litani River (Source: @inon_yttach/8592).

Israel’s security services, together with Shin Bet, killed senior Hamas security officials in Gaza, including Hassan Rabaḥ, a deputy head of the General Security Directorate (Source: @almogboker78/17317).
The IDF reported that its southern command continues operations in Gaza and southern Lebanon to remove immediate threats, as per statements on ongoing engagements (Source: @idf_telegram/24155).
IDF forces in the Gaza Strip killed a terrorist planning imminent attacks and disrupted additional planned attacks within Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24155).
Senior Hamas security figures, including Hassan Rabaḥ and three other officials, were eliminated by Israeli forces in a targeted operation (Source: @almogboker78/17317).
No further specific incidents in the West Bank were detailed in the provided messages.
Hezbollah launched two rockets toward Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; one was intercepted, the other landed near forces without causing casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58408).
Israel’s air force destroyed a Hezbollah engineering unit commander and a rocket launcher in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Following an anti‑tank missile strike that killed an IDF officer north of the Litani River, Israel conducted airstrikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in the area (Source: @inon_yttach/8592).
Civilian evacuation orders were issued for Arnaba, Anqoun, and Kfar Fila in southern Lebanon after Hezbollah violated the cease‑fire (Source: @yediotnews25/130560).
No operational details concerning Syria or Iraq appeared in the sourced messages.
No operational details concerning Gulf states appeared in the sourced messages.

key_developments: IDF eliminated a terrorist planning imminent attacks in Gaza and additional plots inside Israel (Source: @idf_telegram/24155).
Hezbollah fired two rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; one was intercepted, the other landed near forces with no injuries (Source: @manniefabian/58408).
Israel air‑struck Hezbollah engineering unit commander and a rocket launcher in southern Lebanon (Source: @New_security8200/200992).
Sirens warned of rocket fire from Lebanon in multiple Galilee Panhandle communities (Source: @manniefabian/58406).
An anti‑tank missile hit an IDF Golani tank north of the Litani River, killing Capt. Itan Samual Lemberg; Israel responded with airstrikes on Hezbollah sites (Source: @inon_yttach/8592).
Israeli forces killed senior Hamas security officials in Gaza, including deputy head Hassan Rabaḥ (Source: @almogboker78/17317).
Hezbollah denied accusations of targeting UN peacekeeping headquarters, labeling the claim a lie (Source: @presstv/192727).
sections.threat_assessment: Hezbollah continues to launch rockets from southern Lebanon toward Israeli positions, indicating sustained low‑intensity fire despite interceptions. Hamas retains the capability to plan and attempt attacks within Israel, as shown by the thwarted terrorist plot in Gaza. Israeli airstrikes targeting militant leadership and infrastructure suggest a proactive stance to degrade command structures, raising the risk of escalation in both Gaza and the Lebanon border.
sections.regional_response: Lebanese civilian authorities issued evacuation orders for border villages following Hezbollah cease‑fire violations, reflecting heightened local security concerns. Israeli military statements emphasize ongoing operations to eliminate immediate threats across Gaza and southern Lebanon, signaling a continued offensive posture. No direct responses from Iran, Syria, Iraq, or Gulf states were captured in the current set of messages.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of rocket launch sites in southern Lebanon is critical, as Hezbollah retains the ability to fire at Israeli forces with limited interception success. The elimination of senior Hamas security figures may disrupt operational planning in Gaza but could provoke retaliatory attacks. Continued Israeli air operations against Hezbollah infrastructure suggest a focus on degrading rocket launch capabilities, which may lead to further cross‑border incidents.
